label: Prometheus
description: Query and monitor metrics from a Prometheus server using PromQL over HTTP.

form.title: Connect Prometheus
form.subtitle: Point Anton at your Prometheus server URL to run PromQL queries and read metrics.

methods.label: Server URL (no auth)
methods.description: Paste the base URL of your Prometheus server. Use this when your instance is on a private network or already secured at the network layer with no HTTP authentication required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect to Prometheus via Server URL

The simplest path — just tell Anton where your Prometheus HTTP API lives.

### Steps

1. Confirm your Prometheus server is running and reachable from this machine. By default it listens on port `9090`.
2. Find the base URL — usually `http://localhost:9090` for local instances, or `http://<host>:9090` for remote ones.
3. Paste the URL into the **Server URL** field below (no trailing slash needed).
4. Click **Submit**. Anton will probe `<url>/api/v1/status/buildinfo` to verify connectivity.

### Notes

- If your Prometheus sits behind a reverse proxy with a path prefix (e.g. `/prometheus`), include that prefix: `https://example.com/prometheus`.
- For TLS-enabled instances, use `https://` — make sure the certificate is trusted by the system running Anton.
- If your instance requires a username and password, use the **Basic Auth** method instead.

fields.name: server_url
fields.label: Server URL
fields.description: Base URL of your Prometheus server. Anton appends /api/v1/... for all queries.

methods.label: Basic Auth (username + password)
methods.description: Use this when your Prometheus instance or its reverse proxy is protected with HTTP Basic Authentication. Provide the server URL along with your username and password.
methods.how_to:
## Connect to Prometheus with Basic Auth

Some Prometheus deployments sit behind a reverse proxy (nginx, Caddy, etc.) or use Prometheus's built-in `web.config` to enforce HTTP Basic Authentication.

### Steps

1. Locate the base URL of your Prometheus server (e.g. `https://metrics.example.com`).
2. Obtain the **username** and **password** configured for HTTP Basic Auth on the proxy or in `web.yml`.
3. Fill in all three fields below — Server URL, Username, and Password.
4. Click **Submit**. Anton will probe `<url>/api/v1/status/buildinfo` with the provided credentials.

### Setting up Basic Auth on Prometheus (if not done yet)

1. Generate a hashed password using `bcrypt` (e.g. `htpasswd -nB admin`).
2. Create a `web.yml` file:
   ```
   basic_auth_users:
     admin: <bcrypt-hash>
   ```
3. Start Prometheus with `--web.config.file=web.yml`.

### Notes

- Credentials are stored locally by Anton and sent over HTTPS — always use TLS for remote instances.
- If your proxy handles auth separately, the Prometheus config itself does not need `web.yml`.
- See the [Prometheus TLS and auth guide](https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/https/) for full details.

fields.name: server_url
fields.label: Server URL
fields.description: Base URL of your Prometheus server, including scheme and port if non-standard.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: HTTP Basic Auth username configured on your Prometheus server or reverse proxy.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: HTTP Basic Auth password. Stored locally and never sent to Anton's servers.

methods.label: Bearer Token
methods.description: Authenticate using a bearer token — common when Prometheus is fronted by a gateway or deployed on Kubernetes with token-based access control.
methods.how_to:
## Connect to Prometheus with a Bearer Token

Use this method when your Prometheus endpoint requires an `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header — typical for Kubernetes-hosted instances or API gateways.

### Steps

1. Obtain your bearer token from your platform:
   - **Kubernetes**: run `kubectl create token <service-account>` or retrieve a long-lived token from a Secret.
   - **API gateway / proxy**: copy the token from your gateway's dashboard or configuration.
2. Paste the base URL of your Prometheus server into **Server URL**.
3. Paste the token into **Bearer Token** below.
4. Click **Submit**. Anton will probe `<url>/api/v1/status/buildinfo` with the `Authorization` header.

### Notes

- Tokens are stored locally by Anton. Use HTTPS to avoid leaking the token in transit.
- Kubernetes service account tokens may expire — if queries start failing, regenerate and re-paste the token.
- If your instance uses Basic Auth instead, switch to the **Basic Auth** method.

fields.name: server_url
fields.label: Server URL
fields.description: Base URL of your Prometheus server, including scheme and port if non-standard.

fields.name: bearer_token
fields.label: Bearer Token
fields.description: Token sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' with every request. Stored locally.
